ddls:
CREATE TABLE "patients" (
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each patient */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the patient */,
  "dob" TEXT /* Date of birth of the patient */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the patient */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email of the patient */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the patient */,
  "emergency_contact_name" TEXT /* Name of the emergency contact */,
  "emergency_contact_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the emergency contact */,
  "medical_history" TEXT /* Brief medical history of the patient */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the patient record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the patient record */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("patient_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "devices" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of the device (e.g., smartwatch, fitness tracker) */,
  "device_model" TEXT /* Model of the device */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the device */,
  "serial_number" TEXT /* Unique serial number of the device */,
  "firmware_version" TEXT /* Current firmware version of the device */,
  "last_calibration_date" TEXT /* Date of the last calibration of the device */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the device record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the device record */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "biometric_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each recorded data entry */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the patient associated with this data */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the device used to collect this data */,
  "ecg" REAL /* ECG value */,
  "gsr" REAL /* GSR value */,
  "temp" REAL /* Temperature in degrees Celsius */,
  "hr" INTEGER /* Heart rate in beats per minute */,
  "spo2" INTEGER /* Blood oxygen saturation percentage */,
  "timestamp" INTEGER /* Timestamp of the data collection */,
  "session_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the session during which the data was collected */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location where the data was collected */,
  "activity" TEXT /* Activity the patient was engaged in during data collection */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the data was collected */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_biometric_data_patient_id FOREIGN KEY ("patient_id") REFERENCES patients ("patient_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_biometric_data_session_id FOREIGN KEY ("session_id") REFERENCES sessions ("session_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_biometric_data_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "thresholds" (
  "threshold_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each threshold entry */,
  "parameter_name" TEXT /* Name of the biometric parameter (e.g., Temperature, Heart Rate) */,
  "upper_limit" REAL /* Upper limit for the parameter */,
  "lower_limit" REAL /* Lower limit for the parameter */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the patient for whom the threshold is defined */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the threshold was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the threshold */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("threshold_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_thresholds_patient_id FOREIGN KEY ("patient_id") REFERENCES patients ("patient_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the biometric data entry that triggered the alert */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of the alert (e.g., temperature breach, heart rate breach) */,
  "timestamp" INTEGER /* Timestamp of the alert event */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "handled_by" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who handled the alert */,
  "handled_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the alert was handled */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Notes regarding the alert */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the alert was triggered */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES biometric_data ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_handled_by FOREIGN KEY ("handled_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for system access */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for security */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., doctor, admin, patient) */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the user account */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the role was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "patient_device_links" (
  "link_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each link between patient and device */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the patient */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the device */,
  "linked_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the link was created */,
  "unlinked_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the link was removed (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("link_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_patient_device_links_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_patient_device_links_patient_id FOREIGN KEY ("patient_id") REFERENCES patients ("patient_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "health_records" (
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each health record */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the patient this record belongs to */,
  "doctor_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the doctor who created the record */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the record entry */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Notes regarding the patient's health */,
  "vital_signs" TEXT /* Vital signs recorded during the health check */,
  "medications" TEXT /* Medications prescribed or taken by the patient */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the record */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("record_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_health_records_doctor_id FOREIGN KEY ("doctor_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_health_records_patient_id FOREIGN KEY ("patient_id") REFERENCES patients ("patient_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the patient receiving the notification */,
  "message" TEXT /* Content of the notification message */,
  "timestamp" INTEGER /* Timestamp of when the notification was sent */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the notification (e.g., sent, read, dismissed) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the notification was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the notification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_patient_id FOREIGN KEY ("patient_id") REFERENCES patients ("patient_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sessions" (
  "session_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each session */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the patient during the session */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the device used during the session */,
  "start_time" INTEGER /* Start time of the session */,
  "end_time" INTEGER /* End time of the session */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location where the session took place */,
  "activity" TEXT /* Activity the patient was engaged in during the session */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Notes regarding the session */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the session started */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("session_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sessions_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sessions_patient_id FOREIGN KEY ("patient_id") REFERENCES patients ("patient_id")
)
